front.title: Vanta Handshake and Secure Session Specification

abstract.t: This document defines the Vanta authenticated handshake, session establishment, key derivation, and negotiation binding.

section.name: Peer Identity
section.t: Each peer has a long-term Ed25519 identity key pair. The public key bytes are the PeerID.
Implementations MAY use pinned public keys, centralized key registries, or equivalent trust bootstrap mechanisms.

section.name: Handshake Phases
section.t: Application data MUST NOT be exchanged before session activation.

section.name: HELLO
section.t: HELLO exchanges supported protocol versions, crypto suites, maximum frame sizes, transport profiles, and feature sets.

section.name: AUTH_INIT
section.t: AUTH_INIT conveys the initiator identity, ephemeral X25519 public key, freshness data, and a signature over the current handshake transcript and selected parameters.

section.name: AUTH_FINISH
section.t: AUTH_FINISH conveys the responder identity, responder ephemeral key, transcript commitment, and a signature over the handshake transcript.

section.name: Key Derivation
section.t: The shared secret is derived using X25519 over the local private ephemeral key and the remote public ephemeral key.
HKDF-SHA-256 is then used to derive directional session keys, nonce bases, resumption material, audit binding material, and the SessionID.

section.name: Capability and Schema Negotiation
section.t: After peer authentication, both sides exchange schema family support, schema versions, capability sets, extension namespaces, and ordering profiles. No capability may be used before final confirmation.

section.name: Session Activation
section.t: A session becomes active only after successful completion of HELLO, authenticated key exchange, encrypted negotiation, and capability confirmation.

section.name: Active Attack Resistance
section.t: Vanta resists active man-in-the-middle attacks by signing the ephemeral key exchange with long-term identity keys and binding the selected version, suites, and negotiated properties to the authenticated transcript.

section.name: Resumption Foundation
section.t: Vanta defines resumption material for reconnect optimization, but resumption does not imply automatic replay of unacknowledged data. Replay and retransmission remain explicit policy decisions.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: Implementations MUST verify handshake signatures, MUST reject transcript mismatches, and MUST NOT reuse AEAD nonces within the same key scope.

front.title: ChaCha20 and Poly1305 for IETF Protocols
